name: Fleet
backstory:
**Name:** **Orson "Steamheart" Vexley**

**Backstory:**
Orson Vexley is a retired naval engineer who once serviced the great ironclad warships of the old world before the Cataclysm. When the Iron Road rose from the ashes of the industrial age, he saw a new purpose in keeping the mighty locomotives running—partly out of necessity (his pension was worthless in the new economy) and partly because he couldn’t resist the raw power and beauty of steam. Now, he’s the grizzled, no-nonsense overseer of the Iron Road’s fleet, a man who speaks fluent in the language of pistons, boilers, and whistle codes. Rumor has it he once single-handedly stopped a runaway train by leaping onto the cowcatcher mid-route and manually engaging the brake lever, earning him his nickname. He’s got a few tricks up his sleeve, like jury-rigging a broken boiler with spare brass piping or negotiating with rogue tinkerers for "discounted" parts.

**Visual Description:**
Orson is a broad-shouldered man with skin like weathered copper, his face etched with deep lines from years of squinting against the glare of furnace light. His mechanical left arm—a brass-clad prosthetic fitted with a pressure gauge on the wrist—whistles and hisses when he adjusts it, a relic of his old work. He wears a long, oil-stained duster coat over a collared shirt and waistcoat, both patched with mismatched leather and copper rivets. A goggles strap circles his forehead, though he rarely wears them anymore (his vision’s shot from the soot anyway). His tools are always within reach: a wrench dangling from his belt, a pocket watch with a pressure valve for a dial, and a ever-present canteen of boiling water (which he insists is "just tea, don’t let the grease fool ya"). He carries a lantern with a flickering blue flame—his own modification to keep it lit in the wind.

---

**Dialogue Lines:**

1. **"Bah! Don’t tell me about your ‘modern’ electric engines—give me a good, honest steam boiler any day! Why, I’ve seen one of these contraptions run on fumes and a prayer!"** *(Grumbles while tightening a loose valve with a wrench, sending a puff of scalding steam into the air.)*

2. **"You wanna know how to keep this beast from eating ya alive? Treat it like a lady—feed it coal, kiss it with oil, and never, *ever* let it get too thirsty. Now scram before I show ya what happens when a locomotive ‘thirsts’ too long!"** *(Taps the side of the boiler with a sooty hand, glaring at the party with a mix of pride and warning.)*

3. **"Listen here, friend—when the pressure drops below 40 psi, that’s when the real fun starts. You’ll hear the whistle scream like a banshee, the pistons groan like a broken-down sailor on shore leave, and if ya ain’t careful, the whole damn thing’ll shake itself apart. Now, what’s your problem before I lose my temper?"** *(Adjusts his goggles with a grimy finger, eyes fixed on the nearest locomotive with a mix of fondness and exasperation.)*
visual_description: a grizzled steam engineer who maintains the Iron Road locomotive fleet, steampunk aesthetic
